[2018-11-15 23:14] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Each different unit is the "best" in different scenarios. But in a vacuum steel splitters would beat rhinos and tarsiers in any even scenario. And if being built one at a time, they would beat gauss cannons. If it was 10 vs 10 immediately gauss would win though.
[2018-11-15 23:15] jamberine: this vacuum doesn't have defenders though
[2018-11-15 23:15] jamberine: in most prismata situations you can by absorbers and soak
[2018-11-15 23:16] shadourow (Rando): What if we talk about a quantic vacuum ?
[2018-11-15 23:16] shadourow (Rando): Where defenders and anti-defenders can spontaneously appear
[2018-11-15 23:18] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): There are too many scenarios to do effective analyzing. In a purely 1v1 though my money is on splitters. I think my vote for the single best unit in the game though in general are hellhounds.
[2018-11-15 23:18] mrguy888: Arka is clearly the single best unit in the game
[2018-11-15 23:19] mrguy888: hounds are less efficient than drakes
[2018-11-15 23:20] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Is a drake 10 or 12? And drakes require a 5 cost ammunition for full value.
[2018-11-15 23:20] mrguy888: drake is 12 and they dont need to fire to get their threat value
[2018-11-15 23:21] mrguy888: the fact that they get options on top of their efficiency is a net gain
[2018-11-15 23:21] shadourow (Rando): Drake is 12BB for 2 permanent damage and virtually 2 pixies
[2018-11-15 23:21] mrguy888: no better than 2 pixies
[2018-11-15 23:21] mrguy888: you can pop to threaten for more
[2018-11-15 23:22] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): But it actually cost 17 as soon as you click it.
[2018-11-15 23:22] mrguy888: if you pop pixies you cant rethreaten for 0 gold
[2018-11-15 23:22] mrguy888: selling forges that you cant use is free damage for example
[2018-11-15 23:23] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): That's true, but the value goes down compared to other attackers if you dont continue to invest.
[2018-11-15 23:24] shadourow (Rando): That's not how you use drake
[2018-11-15 23:24] mrguy888: No the value goes up since you get a free forge pop lategame
[2018-11-15 23:24] shadourow (Rando): You don't want to refuel drake
[2018-11-15 23:24] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): A Hellhound is a 3:Red: :Blue: for a fast tarsier that you buy an engi with.
[2018-11-15 23:24] jamberine: I think you have the perception that you should click drake every turn and buy back the blastforges
[2018-11-15 23:25] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): I'm saying a 12:Blue: :Blue:  for a 2 damage 4 health attacker isnt that great.
[2018-11-15 23:25] shadourow (Rando): When you have a drake against a monored opponent 🙂
[2018-11-15 23:25] mrguy888: 2 hounds and 1 drake cost the same. they both give 2 damage per turn. They both get 2 bonus crap. Drake bonus crap happens half a turn sooner. Drake also gets the option value of popping forges.
[2018-11-15 23:26] shadourow (Rando): 12 :Blue: :Blue: 2 attackers + 2 instant threat *is* great
[2018-11-15 23:26] shadourow (Rando): The threat matter a lot in non BP matchs
[2018-11-15 23:27] mrguy888: 2 and :Red: :Red: both cost 6 to make per turn. gold is better later red is better earlier.
[2018-11-15 23:27] mrguy888: basically the same cost
[2018-11-15 23:27] shadourow (Rando): (btw, Drake can be powerful and Hellhound be super efficient, that's ok, those units haven't the same purpose)
[2018-11-15 23:27] mrguy888: they basically do have the same purpose though of being efficient attackers
[2018-11-15 23:28] mrguy888: drake is just more efficient but less spammable
[2018-11-15 23:28] shadourow (Rando): Mmmh... Drake is more of a shock unit
[2018-11-15 23:28] mrguy888: in a set with both hound and drake the guy who buys drake and hounds beats the guy who buys just hounds
[2018-11-15 23:29] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Well that's a poor comparison I feel. Saying one buying both good units beats the one buying one good unit.
[2018-11-15 23:29] mrguy888: the guy buying only hounds buys more hounds
[2018-11-15 23:29] mrguy888: if hounds are more efficient than the hounds only guy should win
[2018-11-15 23:30] mrguy888: im saying 1 drake and 2 hounds is better than 4 hounds and that 1 drake is better than 2 hounds.
[2018-11-15 23:30] zera777 (Zera): Hounds give you +1 soak defense, but Drakes threaten +2 damage. If you buy 2 Drakes, your opponent has to build extra defense to account for a possible burst attack.
[2018-11-15 23:30] shadourow (Rando): Welll... that more a proof that Drake and hounds don't have the same purpose
[2018-11-15 23:30] shadourow (Rando): Drake is more than an attacker
[2018-11-15 23:31] zera777 (Zera): The idea is that, by forcing your opponent to build more defense, your Drakes damage their economy just by being built.
[2018-11-15 23:32] mrguy888: yes zera. My point is that drake give threat faster than hounds gives defense and is therefore more efficient since that is the main different.
[2018-11-15 23:32] shadourow (Rando): You damage his build order in general, indeed
[2018-11-15 23:32] f300xen (f0x): drake is actually an 8BB attacker
[2018-11-15 23:32] mrguy888: drake is nutso efficient
[2018-11-15 23:32] zera777 (Zera): In terms of cost/attack, Drake without ability is 2 Steelsplitters, so that's not bad either.
[2018-11-15 23:33] mrguy888: yes that is in fact bad zera
[2018-11-15 23:33] jamberine: wow, 2 of the best base set attacker?

Drakes cant defend and not talking of threat, they're either expensive to click or two splitters with a bit less health that cant block. Hellhound gives you granularity without having to buy it.
[2018-11-15 23:41] jamberine: well the threat kinda requires your opponent to have bought 2 engineers last turn
[2018-11-15 23:41] jamberine: that's an oversimplification
[2018-11-15 23:42] jamberine: but i think it's what mrguy means when he says it gives you 2 stuff half a turn earlier
[2018-11-15 23:42] brute4 (brute): so building drake is how i git gud?
[2018-11-15 23:43] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): You build the things in the set that are best to get gud.
[2018-11-15 23:43] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Each combination radically changes the value of each unit.

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-11-15 to 2018-11-16) ---
[2018-11-15 23:57] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Unrelated, but I love the feeling of when you look at a unit, and it just seems bad to you, but then later you learn how strong it actually is.
[2018-11-15 23:57] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Theres no wild drone emote. :PepeHands:
[2018-11-15 23:58] mrguy888: If only we could drop the word emote from your sentence and have it still be true.
[2018-11-15 23:59] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): is wild drone really that bad
[2018-11-15 23:59] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): I picked up it's one of the least liked units  in the game. Does it just enable too much oppressing strategies?
[2018-11-15 23:59] mrguy888: It is the worst unit in the game by far.
[2018-11-15 23:59] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): (bad as in being a hated unit, not unviable)
[2018-11-16 00:00] shadourow (Rando): yup, everyone hate wild drone
[2018-11-16 00:00] mrguy888: Reduced options means coinflips matters way too much.
[2018-11-16 00:00] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): i dont quite get it. you get to ramp faster, sure, but you also open yourself up to early exploits
[2018-11-16 00:00] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Going first versus second with wild drones in the set is sometimes stupid decisive.
[2018-11-16 00:01] mrguy888: You can normally buy 0, 1 or 2 drones. That gives you 3 different good options to buy tech.
[2018-11-16 00:01] jamberine: I kinda like the decisions that wild drone creates when it doesn't ruin the set though
[2018-11-16 00:02] mrguy888: With wild drone you get to buy 1 wild drone. This gives you 1 good option to buy tech.
[2018-11-16 00:02] mrguy888: And that option is often not the one you want.
[2018-11-16 00:02] shadourow (Rando): When wild drone is in the set, you get the choice to buy it or lose
[2018-11-16 00:02] shadourow (Rando): That's a nice choice !
[2018-11-16 00:02] jamberine: I mean like with snipes and such
[2018-11-16 00:02] mrguy888: vivid drone used to have the same problem.
[2018-11-16 00:03] mrguy888: Then they split it in half like i said to do over a year before they did it and it worked out.
[2018-11-16 00:03] mrguy888: vivid was way worse about it though since it was cheaper and also didnt have counterplay
[2018-11-16 00:04] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Galvani drones and doomed drones are preferred by me as far as what feels healthy for the game.
[2018-11-16 00:06] mrguy888: 1de can be unfair for p1
[2018-11-16 00:06] shadourow (Rando): but that's ok, as 3 engies games are usually P1 favored
[2018-11-16 00:08] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Is it  @mrguy888 ? They have 8 gold turn 2, and can still only get 2 drones..or a combination of more galvanis.
[2018-11-16 00:08] mrguy888: that is a doomed drone opening
[2018-11-16 00:08] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): How can p1 get iDE on 6 gold?
[2018-11-16 00:09] mrguy888: unfair for p1 as in p1 gets shafted
[2018-11-16 00:09] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): P1 can go 11E though.
[2018-11-16 00:10] mrguy888: that is a bad opening though
[2018-11-16 00:11] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): They are half a turn ahead though. The rest of the set could make it worthwhile.
[2018-11-16 00:11] shadourow (Rando): And you lose your doomed drones without getting their soak value
[2018-11-16 00:11] jamberine: doomed drones aren't really more efficient than regular drones when they don't soak on time, or spend energy
[2018-11-16 00:12] jamberine: P1's opening spends gold relatively efficiently already
[2018-11-16 00:12] jamberine: and doesn't waste energy
[2018-11-16 00:13] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): I know it's less efficient to click them on 1 than to soak with them. But tempo is always important, and getting to a certainty point 1 or 2 turns earlier can make a difference. There are lots of times where the soak can be denied from the other units as well. But in general I know getting them too early is bad. It is an option though.
[2018-11-16 00:15] jamberine: can you give an example of getting to a certain point faster with 11E? I can't think of one
[2018-11-16 00:16] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Turn 2 you can DD1, then turn 3 you have 11 income. That's enough for 2 tech buildings
[2018-11-16 00:16] jamberine: you could also go DD/DD though
[2018-11-16 00:17] jamberine: if you want the tech buildings fast you don't want the engineer
[2018-11-16 00:18] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): But your income is lower. What if you just needed that 1 more gold on turn 4 to spend all your tech in an efficient way?
[2018-11-16 00:18] f300xen (f0x): games last long enough for that to be an incredibly shitty play
[2018-11-16 00:19] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Without knowing the extra units that's not something that can be blanketed.
[2018-11-16 00:19] f300xen (f0x): what kind of game has your opponent attacking for 4 by turn 5
[2018-11-16 00:19] shadourow (Rando): that's rude @f0x , that's exactly why players leave the game 😐
[2018-11-16 00:20] jamberine: actually, you should have the same amount of gold at the end of turn 4 right?
[2018-11-16 00:20] jamberine: since you float 2 on turn 2 with DD/DD
[2018-11-16 00:21] mrguy888: 11e doesnt do better at buying tech on turn 3 compared to dd dd and doesnt do better at buying tech on turn 4 than dd dde ddd
[2018-11-16 00:21] artificialhope (Artificial Hope): Oh, if there are perforators, and no good early defense, I buy animus turn 1 P1 and spam A 1 all game.
[2018-11-16 00:22] jamberine: unfortunately wall is in every set

[2018-11-21 00:41] amalloy: @Spyrfyr this is going back to the original article about elyot animus. if the difference between getting unit X now vs half a turn later is small enough, there will be some point at which getting it first leads to a pretty close game
[2018-11-21 00:41] mrguy888: I don't understand your question spirit. You realize the p1 turn 1 savior is the worst possible turn in prismata. Second possible savior is slightly better but still hilariously bad. The longer you wait the better until a certain point where waiting is worse than getting it. That is the same with every unit. First or second is irrelevant. You should be thinking in terms of too early, too late, or the right time.

What Apooche is saying is that the right time sometimes lands squarely on a particular player's half turn. The other player can only get the unit too early or too late and therefore is at a disadvantage.
